title: Lesson 0: Front Matter & Materials Overview   مقدمه
description: Welcome to the CAL Pashto curriculum! This orientation module sets your expectations, outlines the pedagogical philosophy, and introduces the scope of your learning journey.

content.heading: Welcome to the Program
lines: This module functions as your orientation to the Beginning Pashto program.
Our curriculum spans a 28-unit arc (Beginning + Intermediate). Your learning will be supported by a Textbook, Workbook, Tapescript, Teacher's Manual, and a Pashto-English Glossary.

Our target dialect is Kabul/Central Afghan Pashto, as used in official broadcast media. However, Kandahari and Peshawari dialect forms are highly intelligible and will be flagged throughout the course.
Your ultimate proficiency exit target for the full program is ILR 2+ / ACTFL Advanced.

content.text: Script & Grammar Expectations: You will be exposed to dual script (Pashto + Transliteration) in Units 1–3 only. From Unit 2 onward, Pashto script is the primary medium! Additionally, prepare your mind for a major shift: Pashto is strictly an SOV (Subject-Object-Verb) language.

content.heading: Learning Contract & Scope
lines: [Infographic Placeholder: 14 textbook units × ~10 hrs each = ~140 hrs of classroom instruction.]

Throughout this course, you will encounter essential meta-linguistic terminology. Do not be intimidated by terms like 'imperfective', 'perfective', 'ergative', or 'SOV'—we will guide you through them step-by-step.

content.heading: The Romanisation Key
lines: Before we dive into the Pashto alphabet in Lesson 1, familiarize yourself with the IPA-adjacent romanisation key we use for pronunciation.
Pay special attention to unique notation for Pashto sounds:

content.heading: Cultural Foundations
lines: Language cannot be separated from culture. Throughout this course, your dialogues and interactions will be framed by 'Pashtunwali'—the traditional Pashtun code of ethics.
You will notice register differences between formal Kabuli media Pashto and rural or tribal varieties.
Crucially, male-female interaction norms deeply affect how dialogues are designed and how you should speak in authentic contexts.

content.question: According to the curriculum's script policy, when are learners expected to transition to reading primarily in the Pashto script, with transliteration used only for introducing new vocabulary?
options: Never; transliteration is always the primary text.
content.explanation: Transliteration acts as scaffolding in Units 1–3. After Unit 3, Pashto script becomes the primary reading medium, and transliteration is only used when introducing brand-new vocabulary.
